title: Hyundai Workers Strike Over New Humanoid Robots
subtitle: Thousands of employees in South Korea take action as the car industry faces a new era of automation.

summary.en: Thousands of Hyundai workers in South Korea have started strikes to protest the use of humanoid robots. The company plans to use thousands of Atlas robots in its factories to perform manual labor. Workers worry that these machines will replace their jobs or lower their pay. Unions are asking for better job security and higher retirement ages to protect their members. Experts suggest that these robots could become cheaper than human labor in the near future.
summary.zh: 南韓數千名現代汽車員工已發起罷工，抗議公司使用人形機器人。該公司計畫在工廠中使用數千台亞特拉斯機器人來執行體力勞動。員工擔心這些機器將取代他們的工作或降低薪資。工會正要求更好的工作保障與提高退休年齡，以保護會員權益。專家指出，這些機器人在不久的將來可能比人力更便宜。

paragraphs.en: Thousands of workers at a major Hyundai car factory in Ulsan, South Korea, have started walking off the job early. This action is a response to the company’s plans to use humanoid robots—machines designed to look and move like humans—on the production line. According to The Wall Street Journal, this is the first time in the car industry that workers have gone on strike specifically because of humanoid robots.
paragraphs.zh: 南韓蔚山一家現代汽車（Hyundai）主要工廠的數千名員工，已開始提前離崗抗議。此舉是為了回應公司計畫在生產線上使用人形機器人——即設計外觀與動作皆仿照人類的機器。根據《華爾街日報》（The Wall Street Journal）報導，這是汽車產業首次有員工因人形機器人而發起罷工。

paragraphs.en: The trouble began after 15 rounds of talks between the company and the labor union failed to reach an agreement. Reports from The Korea Times state that workers ended their shifts two hours early for several days in mid-July. They also plan to hold four-hour strikes later in the month to show their frustration. The union represents more than 39,000 workers who are worried about their future in a factory that is becoming more automated.
paragraphs.zh: 這場紛爭始於公司與工會進行十五輪談判後仍無法達成共識。據《韓國時報》（The Korea Times）報導，工人在七月中旬連續幾天提前兩小時結束輪班。他們也計畫在該月稍晚舉行四小時的罷工，以表達不滿。該工會代表超過三萬九千名員工，他們對於工廠日益自動化後的未來感到擔憂。

paragraphs.en: The tension started when Hyundai Motor Group showed off the newest version of the Atlas robot earlier this year. Atlas is a tall, two-legged robot made by Boston Dynamics, a company that Hyundai is in the process of buying completely. The robot is strong enough to lift heavy objects, and Hyundai has big plans for it. According to The Korea Herald, the company wants to put more than 25,000 of these robots into its factories. While the first robots are expected to arrive in factories in the United States by 2028, the company has not yet said exactly when they will arrive in other locations.
paragraphs.zh: 緊張局勢始於現代汽車集團（Hyundai Motor Group）今年稍早展示了最新版本的「亞特拉斯」（Atlas）機器人。亞特拉斯是由波士頓動力（Boston Dynamics）製造的一款高大雙足機器人，現代汽車目前正計畫將該公司完全收購。這款機器人強壯到足以搬運重物，而現代汽車對其有宏大的規劃。根據《韓國先驅報》（The Korea Herald）報導，公司希望在工廠中部署超過兩萬五千台這類機器人。雖然首批機器人預計於二零二八年前抵達美國工廠，但公司尚未說明何時會引進至其他地區。

paragraphs.en: For the company, the robots are a way to work more efficiently. According to an interview with Bloomberg, analyst Esther Yim from Samsung Securities Co. noted that each Atlas robot costs about $130,000. However, she believes the robots will pay for themselves within two years. Other experts see even more potential for cost savings. James Hong at Macquarie Securities Korea Ltd. suggested that if the price of the robots drops to $100,000, the cost to run them could be lower than the current US federal minimum wage. This makes the robots much cheaper than paying a human worker a full salary.
paragraphs.zh: 對公司而言，這些機器人是提升工作效率的途徑。根據彭博社（Bloomberg）的訪談，三星證券（Samsung Securities Co.）的分析師林艾絲特（Esther Yim）指出，每台亞特拉斯機器人的成本約為十三萬美元。然而，她認為這些機器人能在兩年內回本。其他專家則看見了更大的成本節省潛力。麥格理證券韓國分公司（Macquarie Securities Korea Ltd.）的洪詹姆斯（James Hong）表示，如果機器人價格降至十萬美元，其運作成本可能低於目前的美國聯邦最低工資。這使得機器人比支付人類員工全額薪資便宜得多。

paragraphs.en: Because of these concerns, the union is fighting for new protections. The Wall Street Journal reported that the union wants Hyundai to change how workers are paid. They are asking for a fixed salary instead of hourly pay to make sure that their income does not drop if the company reduces their working hours. Additionally, the union is asking the company to raise the retirement age from 60 to 65. They are also looking for larger bonuses for the workers who stay on the job.
paragraphs.zh: 由於這些顧慮，工會正爭取新的保障措施。《華爾街日報》報導指出，工會希望現代汽車改變薪資支付方式。他們要求改為固定薪資而非時薪，以確保若公司縮減工時，員工收入不會減少。此外，工會要求公司將退休年齡從六十歲提高至六十五歲，並為留任員工爭取更高的獎金。

paragraphs.en: This situation highlights a growing debate about the role of technology in the workplace. While companies see robots as a way to increase production and lower costs, workers see them as a threat to their livelihoods. As humanoid robots become more capable, the pressure on companies to balance new technology with the needs of their human employees will likely increase. For now, the workers at the Ulsan plant are waiting to see if Hyundai will listen to their demands before more robots are introduced to the factory floor.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況凸顯了關於科技在職場中扮演角色的日益激烈的辯論。雖然企業將機器人視為提高產量與降低成本的手段，但員工卻將其視為對生計的威脅。隨著人形機器人能力愈發強大，企業在平衡新科技與人類員工需求方面所面臨的壓力可能會隨之增加。目前，蔚山工廠的員工正等待現代汽車在引進更多機器人至生產線前，是否會回應他們的訴求。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To leave one's place of work, especially as a form of protest or strike.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （因抗議或罷工）離開工作崗位。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「走路離開」，這裡指罷工行為。文中：Thousands of workers at a major Hyundai car factory in Ulsan, South Korea, have started walking off the job early.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To display something proudly to others.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「愛現的人」，這裡作動詞片語，指公開展示新產品。文中：The tension started when Hyundai Motor Group showed off the newest version of the Atlas robot earlier this year.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To earn back the money that was spent on them through savings or increased profit.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 回本；透過節省成本或增加獲利賺回當初的投資。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「為自己付錢」，這裡指投資的機器能透過節省成本來回本。文中：However, she believes the robots will pay for themselves within two years.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory

quiz.question: What specific change is the labor union demanding regarding their compensation structure?

options.A: They want a pay increase based on the number of robots installed.
options.B: They want to switch from hourly wages to a fixed salary.
options.C: They want the company to pay for their early retirement at age 60.
options.D: They want to be paid in bonuses rather than a base salary.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，工會要求將薪資結構從時薪制改為固定薪資制（fixed salary），以確保公司減少工時時，員工的收入不會受到影響。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章未提及依據機器人數量加薪；選項 C 錯誤，因為工會是要求將退休年齡從 60 歲提高到 65 歲，而非要求提前退休；選項 D 錯誤，工會雖然有提到獎金，但主要訴求是薪資結構的改變，而非完全以獎金取代底薪。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why are workers particularly concerned about the introduction of Atlas robots?

options.A: They believe the robots will cause safety hazards on the production line.
options.B: They fear the robots will be used to spy on their private activities.
options.C: They anticipate that robots will eventually replace human labor and reduce job security.
options.D: They are upset that the robots are being purchased from a foreign company.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第二段提到工會代表 39,000 名擔心工廠自動化後未來發展的員工，且第四段分析師指出機器人的運行成本甚至低於美國聯邦最低工資。綜合這些資訊，可以推論工會擔憂的是機器人將取代人力，導致工作機會減少或生存受到威脅。選項 A 並未在文中提到；選項 B 為過度延伸的猜測；選項 D 雖然提到 Boston Dynamics 是美國公司，但這並非工會抗議的核心原因。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'pay for themselves' mean?

options.A: The robots will generate enough savings or profit to cover their initial cost.
options.B: The robots will be given to the company for free after two years.
options.C: The robots will start working without needing any human supervision.
options.D: The robots will be able to repair their own technical malfunctions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此上下文中，「pay for themselves」是指投資成本透過節省的人力開支或生產效率提升，在一段時間後達到損益平衡，即「回本」。選項 B 錯誤，這不是免費贈送；選項 C 與自動化有關但非該片語意義；選項 D 則是關於機器人維修，與投資回報無關。此片語在商業語境中常用於描述資本支出的回收。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: A detailed technical review of the newest Atlas humanoid robot features.
options.B: A report on the financial success of Boston Dynamics in the global market.
options.C: An analysis of the conflict between corporate automation goals and worker job security.
options.D: A guide on how to negotiate labor contracts in the South Korean automotive industry.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心圍繞在現代汽車工廠引入人形機器人所引發的勞資爭議，探討公司追求效率與勞工維護生計之間的矛盾。文章開頭介紹罷工事件，中間分析經濟誘因，最後討論勞工訴求，皆是為了支持這個核心議題。選項 A 過於細節；選項 B 非文章重點；選項 D 則過於狹隘，本文並非教學指南，而是新聞報導。
